sections: Handling Each Type of Antique on Its Own Terms

No two antiques behave the same way under transit, which is why our specialist team assesses every piece individually before a single item leaves the property. A longcase clock, for example, must never be moved upright with its pendulum and weights still hanging — the suspension spring is a fragile sliver of brass that snaps under the slightest jolt. We dismantle the movement, secure the pendulum and weights separately, immobilise the bob, and pad the hood and trunk independently so the case and mechanism travel without stress. Marble tops and sculpture are deceptively vulnerable; although the stone feels solid, it shears along hidden veins, so we always transport marble on edge rather than flat, fully supported on padded battens, never laid down where its own weight can crack it. Gilt frames demand a no-contact approach because the water-gilded gesso lifts at the lightest touch of bare skin, so we handle them in cotton gloves and corner-protect the ornamentation before soft-wrapping. From chandeliers stripped of their lustres and catalogued drop by drop, to ceramics nested in custom foam, to oil paintings faced and travel-framed against canvas flex, each category follows its own protocol. If you are planning a move within the county, our dedicated antiques moving service in West Sussex page sets out exactly how this local expertise is delivered.
Condition Reports & Inventories Before Anything Moves

The single most valuable thing we do before lifting a finger is document. A written condition report paired with a photographic inventory protects both you and us, establishing the precise state of every item the moment it enters our care — existing hairlines in glaze, a slight bow in a frame, a touch of foxing on a watercolour, a previous repair to a chair stretcher. For collections of any size, this record is the backbone of a smooth move and the foundation of any future quotation for your antiques move. We capture detail at a level that stands up to the scrutiny of insurers, auctioneers and estate executors alike.

  • High-resolution photographs of each item from multiple angles, including any pre-existing marks or restoration
  • A written description noting maker, period, materials and dimensions where known
  • Notes on structural quirks — loose veneer, stress on joints, weak hinges — that dictate the handling method
  • A logged checklist used at both collection and delivery so nothing is overlooked in transit
Soft-Wrapping or Bespoke Crating — Choosing the Right Protection

Not every antique needs a crate, and not every antique is safe without one. The skill lies in matching the protection to the object. For robust case furniture travelling a short distance in a dedicated, blanket-lined vehicle, expert soft-wrapping in clean export blankets, acid-free tissue and breathable covers is often the most appropriate method — it cushions, it breathes, and it avoids trapping moisture against polished surfaces. But for the genuinely fragile, the irreplaceable or anything bound for a long journey, a bespoke crate built to the exact dimensions of the piece is the gold standard. We build timber crates lined with high-density foam that immobilise the contents completely, isolating them from shock and vibration. Our custom crate service and our fragile packing specialists work hand in hand so that mirrors, marble, glass cabinets and gilded objects each receive precisely the casing they require. When the piece is destined for shipping overseas, our export packing service adds vapour barriers and case-hardened construction to meet international transit demands.
Two-Person Handling, Lifting Technique & the White-Glove Standard

Weight is rarely the real danger when moving antiques — balance, leverage and grip are. A gilt console table is light but catastrophically front-heavy; a marble-topped washstand will tip the instant the top shifts; a glazed bookcase can rack and crack its panes if lifted unevenly. For this reason every piece of consequence is handled by a minimum of two trained porters working in unison, communicating each movement and taking the load through the structural points of the item rather than its decorative extremities. We never carry furniture by its galleries, finials or handles. Stairs, tight doorways and period thresholds are negotiated slowly with one person always controlling the descending end. This disciplined, unhurried approach is the heart of our white-glove handling service, where placement, unwrapping and final positioning in the new home are all part of the job — not an afterthought. You can read more about the people behind this standard on our about us page.
Deliveries to Auction Houses, Fairs & Dealers

A great deal of our work involves moving pieces not from one home to another but into the trade itself — consignments to auction houses ahead of a sale, stock delivered to antiques fairs on tight set-up schedules, and acquisitions transported between dealers. These jobs carry their own pressures: fixed delivery windows, loading-bay restrictions, and the expectation that goods arrive sale-ready and pristine. We coordinate directly with saleroom porters and fair organisers, arrive within the booked slot, and unload in a manner that respects both the venue and the value passing through it. Because we are a LAPADA member — the Association of Art & Antiques Dealers — we understand the conventions and the paperwork of the trade from the inside, which is precisely why we are recommended by names such as Fine & Country, Justin Lloyd and Mansell McTaggart. Should a piece need to be held between collection and a future sale, our secure antiques storage facilities bridge the gap in clean, monitored conditions.
What LAPADA Accreditation & Full Insurance Mean for You

When you entrust a collection to us, two assurances matter most: who we are and what happens if the worst occurs. Our membership of LAPADA is not a logo for show — it signals that we operate to the standards expected within the art and antiques trade, the same world our dealer clients inhabit every day. Alongside this we are Checkatrade-verified, so our track record is independently visible, and we are fully insured with £10m liability cover, giving genuine financial protection to high-value items while they are in our hands. As a family-run Sussex firm based at Doryln House, London Road, Ashington, near Pulborough, we have built this business since 2016 on careful, personal service across West and East Sussex, Surrey, Hampshire and Kent, as well as wider UK and European moves.

  • LAPADA member — recognised within the Association of Art & Antiques Dealers
  • Checkatrade-verified — an independently reviewed, accountable reputation
  • Fully insured with £10m liability cover for genuine peace of mind
  • Family-run since 2016, covering Sussex, Surrey, Hampshire, Kent and beyond
faqs: How should I prepare a valuable collection before the move?

The best preparation is to leave the pieces exactly as they are and let us assess them in situ. Resist the urge to wrap items yourself, as well-meant cling film and bubble wrap can mark polished surfaces and trap damaging moisture. Gather any provenance, valuations or restoration records you hold so they can sit alongside our condition report, and tell us about any item with a known weakness. We will then build the right packing and handling plan around your collection. To begin, simply request a quote for your antiques move and we will arrange a survey.
Do you crate every antique, or is soft-wrapping ever enough?

We choose the method to suit the object and the journey. Robust case furniture moving a short distance in a dedicated, blanket-lined van is often best protected by expert soft-wrapping, while fragile, irreplaceable or long-distance pieces are far safer in a bespoke crate built to their exact size. Our custom crate service and fragile packing team assess each item so you are never paying for protection you do not need, nor risking damage by under-packing something precious.
Can you deliver consigned pieces to an auction house or fair?

Yes. A significant part of our work is trade delivery — consignments to salerooms, stock to antiques fairs, and pieces moving between dealers. We work to booked delivery windows, coordinate with venue porters, and arrive with goods presentation-ready. As a LAPADA member we understand the trade’s conventions first-hand, which is why dealers and agents recommend us.
What does your £10m insurance actually cover during a move?

Our £10m liability cover provides financial protection for high-value items while they are in our care during handling, transit and storage. Combined with the detailed condition report and photographic inventory we complete before anything moves, it gives a clear, documented basis for peace of mind throughout the job. If you would like the specifics in writing for a particular collection, contact us on 01903 893731 or contact@wolves-removals.co.uk and we will confirm the cover in full.
